title: EccKey creation flushes SRK on partial failure
description: In EccKey::create, if step 2 or step 3 fails after the SRK has already been created, the code explicitly calls wolfTPM2_UnloadHandle on the SRK before returning the error. Without this, a failed key-creation attempt would leak the SRK transient slot on the TPM. Transient slots are scarce and the error path is exactly where a maintainer is least likely to think about cleanup. Pattern: create resource, on error in subsequent step, free resource then propagate error. Alternative rejected: a RAII guard struct was considered but adds complexity; explicit flush before return is simpler and equally correct since the SRK is not yet wrapped in a safe Rust type at that point.
